findings.summary: Exploration sub-components depend on the wide QueryExecutor interface instead of narrow SPI ports
evidence: SingleSeedExplorer.java line 37: constructor takes QueryExecutor (the wide interface extending Closeable, CollocateQueryPort, SketchQueryPort, plus executeCqlQuery, executeDependencyPattern, getTotalFrequency)
MultiSeedExplorer.java line 35: 'public MultiSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)' — same wide dependency
CollocateProfileComparator.java line 36: 'public CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor, ...)' — same wide dependency
In contrast, api handlers explicitly use narrow ports: SketchHandlers takes SketchQueryPort (line 42), ConcordanceHandlers takes CollocateQueryPort, CorpusQueryHandlers takes CollocateQueryPort
The executeCollocations method used by exploration classes is defined only on QueryExecutor, not on any SPI port — forcing exploration to import the wide interface
findings.suggestion: Extract an ExplorationQueryPort interface (or extend an existing SPI) that exposes only executeCollocations and executeSurfaceCollocations. Declare SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, and CollocateProfileComparator against this narrower type, mirroring the SPI discipline already applied in the api layer.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc claims 'thin facade' but the class owns non-trivial logic beyond delegation
evidence: Class Javadoc (line ~42): 'Thin facade wiring SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, and CollocateProfileComparator for the HTTP exploration layer'
fetchExamples() (line ~170): implements deduplication loop with HashSet<String> seen — not a delegation; this is owned business logic
exploreByRelation() (line ~134): validates collocatePosGroup, throws IllegalArgumentException for OTHER group — an ownership decision, not a passthrough
All 4 service methods wrap IOException in ExplorationException — owned translation logic across the class
Three public constructors (convenience, full-injection, test bypass) plus an internal GrammarConfig-to-nounCqlPattern translation in the full-injection constructor
findings.suggestion: Either rename/redescribe the class as a service class (e.g. 'SemanticFieldService') that owns deduplication, validation, and error-wrapping concerns, or extract the non-delegation logic into helpers so the facade claim becomes accurate.

findings.summary: MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator declare public constructors/methods on package-private classes, while SingleSeedExplorer correctly uses package-private visibility throughout
evidence: MultiSeedExplorer.java line 31: 'class MultiSeedExplorer' (package-private), line 35: 'public MultiSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)' — public constructor

CollocateProfileComparator.java line 27: 'class CollocateProfileComparator' (package-private), line 36: 'public CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor, GrammarConfig grammarConfig)' — public constructor; line 54: 'public ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(...)' — public method
SingleSeedExplorer.java line 34: 'class SingleSeedExplorer' (package-private), line 41: 'SingleSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, String nounCqlPattern)' — package-private constructor (no modifier)
On a package-private class, 'public' on members has no practical effect outside the package — the modifier is misleading about the intended access level
findings.suggestion: Remove the 'public' modifier from MultiSeedExplorer's constructor and CollocateProfileComparator's constructor and methods, making them package-private to match SingleSeedExplorer's convention and accurately signal that these are internal algorithm classes.

findings.summary: executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern silently return empty list for null/empty lemma, while executeSurfaceCollocations throws IllegalArgumentException when the lemma is absent from the pattern
evidence: QueryExecutor.java line 36 Javadoc on executeCollocations: 'Returns an empty list silently if null or empty'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line 97-103: 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { logger.debug("..."); return Collections.emptyList(); }'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line ~153: executeDependencyPattern also guards 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { return Collections.emptyList(); }'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line ~198: executeSurfaceCollocations throws IllegalArgumentException: 'lemma not extractable from pattern — ... (got: ' + bcqlPattern + ')'
All three methods are surface-query variants in the same interface; callers expecting consistent behavior must handle both the silent-empty and the throw cases
findings.suggestion: Unify the missing-lemma contract across all three methods: either (a) make executeSurfaceCollocations return an empty list when no lemma attribute is present (consistent with the null-lemma behavior of siblings), or (b) change executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern to throw IllegalArgumentException on null/empty lemma (consistent with executeSurfaceCollocations). Document the chosen contract in the QueryExecutor interface Javadoc.

findings.summary: SketchHandlers.handleRelationQueryForPattern dispatches by relation ID, not by pattern — the 'ForPattern' suffix misrepresents the routing key
evidence: SketchHandlers.java line 171: 'private void handleRelationQueryForPattern(HttpExchange exchange, String lemma, String relationId, RelationType relationType)'
The parameter is named 'relationId', not 'pattern' — the method looks up a RelationConfig by ID and dispatches the query
Sibling methods handleFullSketchForType, handleDependencySketch all use type-based names that accurately describe their dispatch key
Callers (line 62, 72) pass a deprel string or relation string parsed from the URL path segment — these are IDs, not patterns
findings.suggestion: Rename to handleNamedRelationQuery(HttpExchange, String lemma, String relationId, RelationType) to match the actual dispatch semantics and align with sibling method naming.

findings.summary: SnippetResult (the basic concordance hit without collocate extraction) is named for a text artifact rather than its role as the non-collocate variant of ConcordanceResult
evidence: ConcordanceResult.java line 7-8: 'public sealed interface ConcordanceResult permits SnippetResult, CollocateResult' — two permitted subtypes
SnippetResult.java: 'record SnippetResult(String sentence, int startOffset, int endOffset, String docId)' — no collocateLemma, frequency, or logDice fields
CollocateResult.java: 'record CollocateResult(String sentence, String rawXml, int startOffset, int endOffset, String docId, String collocateLemma, long frequency, double logDice)'

The distinction between the two is that SnippetResult is a raw concordance hit (from executeCqlQuery) while CollocateResult is a scored/collocated hit. The name 'Snippet' does not communicate this distinction — it sounds like a display artifact rather than a query result type.
findings.suggestion: Rename SnippetResult to RawConcordanceResult or BasicHitResult to make the contrast with CollocateResult self-evident: one is a plain position-marked concordance, the other carries collocate scoring data.

findings.summary: RelationUtils bundles 6 unrelated concerns into a single static utility class
evidence: resolveRelationAlias / validateAliases — alias lifecycle management
findBestCollocatePattern — grammar-config search
buildFullPattern (2 overloads) — BCQL pattern construction
buildCollocateReversePattern — reverse-lookup pattern derivation
computeCollocatePosGroup + extractLabelContent + resolvePosGroupFromPrefix — POS group inference from raw pattern strings
findings.suggestion: Extract the POS-group inference helpers (computeCollocatePosGroup, extractLabelContent, resolvePosGroupFromPrefix) into a dedicated package-private class (e.g. CollocatePosResolver). Consider splitting alias management and pattern building into separate final classes so each has a single coherent responsibility.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer stores executor directly but only uses it in fetchExamples; all other methods delegate to sub-objects that each hold their own reference to the same executor
evidence: Field: private final QueryExecutor executor (line 54)
executor is passed to CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer, SingleSeedExplorer — each stores it internally
Only fetchExamples (line 174) calls executor.executeBcqlQuery directly on the field
exploreByRelation, exploreMultiSeed, compareCollocateProfiles never reference this.executor
findings.suggestion: Move the executeBcqlQuery call in fetchExamples to a dedicated collaborator (e.g. ExamplesQueryService) that accepts CollocateQueryPort, then remove the executor field from SemanticFieldExplorer. This eliminates the phantom shared state and makes each dependency's scope explicit.

findings.summary: executeCollocateSearchWithStoredFields and executeCollocateSearch are thin wrappers that differ only by a single boolean flag, leaking an implementation detail into the public method names
evidence: executeCollocateSearchWithStoredFields delegates to performCollocateSearch(lemma, pattern, true)
executeCollocateSearch delegates to performCollocateSearch(lemma, pattern, false)
Both public methods have no body other than the delegation
findings.suggestion: Either collapse to a single method accepting an explicit CollocateSearchMode enum, or inline the two-line wrapper bodies at call sites in BlackLabQueryExecutor to remove the indirection tier.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer (and its sub-objects) depend on QueryExecutor (full interface) when only CollocateQueryPort is needed for the one direct call in fetchExamples
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer constructor accepts QueryExecutor executor
fetchExamples only calls executor.executeBcqlQuery() — declared on CollocateQueryPort
ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers already follow the narrow-port pattern by depending on CollocateQueryPort
The exploration layer thus has a wider dependency surface than its actual usage requires
findings.suggestion: Widen the exploration layer's dependency surface only as needed: accept CollocateQueryPort for corpus-lookup operations. This mirrors the pattern ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers already follow and makes the dependency boundary explicit.

findings.summary: CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex makes two separate passes through the pattern string: one indexOf call to find the label, then a full character scan from position 0 to count bracket tokens
evidence: int labelIndex = pattern.indexOf(labelStr) — first pass to locate label
for (int i = 0; i < pattern.length(); i++) — second pass from position 0 counting '[' characters
The token counter restarts from 0 rather than walking forward from labelIndex
findings.suggestion: Combine into a single forward pass: count '[' characters incrementally while scanning toward labelIndex; stop as soon as the matching bracket is found at or after labelIndex. This eliminates the redundant full-string re-scan.

findings.summary: executeSurfaceCollocations is declared in both SketchQueryPort and QueryExecutor (which extends it), resulting in duplicate signatures with divergent Javadoc
evidence: SketchQueryPort.executeSurfaceCollocations — concise declaration at line 30
QueryExecutor.executeSurfaceCollocations — re-declared at line 87 with a longer @implNote comment
QueryExecutor already extends SketchQueryPort, so the re-declaration is redundant
findings.suggestion: Remove the re-declaration from QueryExecutor; move any additional Javadoc into the SketchQueryPort definition. If the extended documentation is important, annotate with @see or link to SketchQueryPort from the QueryExecutor interface-level Javadoc.

findings.summary: ExplorationHandlers error cases — ExplorationException → 503, unknown relation → 400 — are not tested through the HTTP stack
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.sendExplorationError maps ExplorationException → 503 but no test injects a failing service
resolveRelationConfig throws IAE for unknown relation IDs but no test exercises this path via handleSemanticFieldExplore
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: no test for seeds.size() < 2 case through the HTTP handler (only service-level validation is tested)
findings.suggestion: Add tests that inject a stub ExplorationService that throws ExplorationException and verify the response is 503. Add tests for unknown relation IDs and single-seed calls to the multi endpoint and verify 400 responses.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorerTest defines a local StubExecutor subclass that duplicates the shared StubQueryExecutor infrastructure
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorerTest defines `private static class StubExecutor extends StubQueryExecutor` with duplicate override pattern
StubQueryExecutor's Javadoc explicitly documents the anonymous-subclass override pattern as the intended test use
Other test files (ExplorationHandlersTest, SketchHandlersTest) use anonymous subclasses of StubQueryExecutor correctly
findings.suggestion: Remove the local StubExecutor class; replace usages with anonymous subclasses of StubQueryExecutor following the pattern already used in ExplorationHandlersTest.collocatingExecutor.

findings.summary: CLAUDE.md code structure map lists multiple classes at wrong paths or that no longer exist
evidence: CLAUDE.md line 111 lists `api/GrammarConfigSerializer.java` — this class exists only in `src/test/`, not in production sources
CLAUDE.md line 124 lists `exploration/ExplorationService.java` — the actual file is at `exploration/spi/ExplorationService.java`
CLAUDE.md line 133 lists `model/FetchExamplesOptions.java` — the actual file is at `model/exploration/FetchExamplesOptions.java`
CLAUDE.md line 135 lists `model/QueryResults.java` — this file does not exist; result types live under `model/sketch/`
CLAUDE.md line 136 lists `model/RelationType.java` — the actual file is at `config/RelationType.java`
CLAUDE.md lines 157-159 list `viz/Edge.java` and `viz/RelationEdgeType.java` — both actually live under `model/exploration/`
CLAUDE.md line 120 lists `config/RelationPatternBuilder.java` — this file does not exist
CLAUDE.md omits `api/ComparisonResponseAssembler.java`, `api/SketchResponseAssembler.java`, and the entire `query/spi/` package

An older spec section within CLAUDE.md (visible in the embedded concept-sketch-spec content) still references removed packages `grammar/` and `tagging/` and non-existent classes CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLToLuceneCompiler, WordSketchQueryExecutor, SimpleTagger, LongIntHashMap, CQLVerifier — none of which exist in the current codebase
CLAUDE.md performance-tuning section references `CollocationsBuilderV2` and `HybridLuceneIndexer` — neither exists
findings.suggestion: Regenerate the `## Code Structure` section of CLAUDE.md by walking `src/main/java` and updating all class paths, removing non-existent entries, and adding the missing classes. Remove or clearly archive the stale spec content that references the old `grammar/` and `tagging/` packages.

findings.summary: Two endpoints serve concordance examples under different URL prefixes with diverging error semantics
evidence: GET /api/semantic-field/examples returns 503 ExplorationException if the corpus fails; GET /api/concordance/examples returns 500 IOException for the same failure

GET /api/concordance/examples silently falls back to a proximity pattern when the relation is unknown (returns fallback:true in body); GET /api/semantic-field/examples throws 400 for the same condition — a caller switching between the two endpoints gets different behavior for the same invalid relation

Both endpoints accept identical parameter names (seed, collocate, relation, top) and return the same ExamplesResponse record, making the URL-level split invisible to consumers who must also memorise the diverging error contracts
findings.suggestion: Document the intended difference explicitly at the HTTP level — either via distinct error codes (e.g., 422 for unknown relation vs 503 for corpus failure) or by consolidating the two endpoints behind a single path with a `mode=semantic|concordance` parameter. If the fallback behaviour in the concordance endpoint is intentional, add a clear note in both Javadocs.

findings.summary: The `dep` keyword is overloaded as both a sketch-type discriminator and a URL path segment in /api/sketch/
evidence: routeSketchRequest splits the path and checks if parts[1].equals(RelationType.DEP.label()) — meaning a lemma named exactly `dep` would be misrouted to the dependency sketch handler instead of the single-relation surface handler
The server Javadoc lists both `/api/sketch/{lemma}/dep` and `/api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation}` but does not document the collision risk or that `dep` is a reserved segment
The relation ID namespace and the `dep` sketch-type keyword share the same URL position without a type prefix (e.g., `/api/sketch/{lemma}/type/dep` or a query param)
findings.suggestion: Reserve the `dep` path segment explicitly in Javadoc and validate that no grammar relation can have id=`dep`. Alternatively, move the sketch-type discriminator to a query parameter (`?type=dep`) to eliminate the ambiguity entirely.

findings.summary: PosGroup (domain enum) lives in model/ while the analogous RelationType enum lives in config/
evidence: PosGroup is a domain enum (NOUN, VERB, ADJ, ADV, OTHER) used by SemanticFieldExplorer and RelationUtils — it lives in the model/ root package
RelationType (SURFACE, DEP) is also a domain enum used by SketchHandlers and GrammarConfig — it lives in config/
Both enums are used across the config/, exploration/, and api/ layers but are homed in different packages with no documented rationale for the asymmetry
findings.suggestion: Move both enums to a single location — either both in config/ (since they model grammar-level distinctions) or both in model/ (since they represent domain classifications). Document the chosen convention in the model/ or config/ package-info.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer package-private test constructor accepts executor/nounCqlPattern without null validation, unlike the production injection constructor
evidence: Test constructor (line 70): `SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, String nounCqlPattern)` has no Objects.requireNonNull calls and no @NonNull annotations on parameters

Production injection constructor (lines 90-100): `Objects.requireNonNull(executor, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(comparator, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(multiSeedExplorer, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(grammarConfig, ...)` are all present

A null executor passed to the test constructor is silently stored and propagated into CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer, and SingleSeedExplorer, where it will cause an NPE at query time with no descriptive message
findings.suggestion: Add explicit null guards in the test constructor: `Objects.requireNonNull(executor, "executor must not be null")` and `Objects.requireNonNull(nounCqlPattern, "nounCqlPattern must not be null")`. Add @NonNull annotations to both parameters to make the contract visible.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer stores a QueryExecutor field used only in fetchExamples, while exploreByRelation/exploreMultiSeed/compareCollocateProfiles all fully delegate to dedicated sub-components
evidence: `exploreByRelation` delegates entirely to `singleSeedExplorer.explore(...)`
`exploreMultiSeed` delegates entirely to `multiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection(...)`
`compareCollocateProfiles` delegates entirely to `comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(...)`
`fetchExamples` (lines 167-193) is directly implemented on SemanticFieldExplorer itself via `executor.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlQuery, maxExamples)` — breaking the uniform delegation/facade pattern
Private field `private final QueryExecutor executor` (line 54) exists solely to service `fetchExamples`; all other delegation paths never touch it
findings.suggestion: Extract the examples-fetch logic into a dedicated `ExamplesFetcher` package-private class (parallel to SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) and delegate from SemanticFieldExplorer. This makes the facade pattern consistent across all four service methods and removes the dual role of the class.

findings.summary: ExplorationService interface Javadoc @throws ExplorationException for all four methods, but no interface method signature declares it; SemanticFieldExplorer implementation does declare it — asymmetric contract visibility
evidence: ExplorationService.exploreByRelation (line 36-40): no `throws` clause in signature, but Javadoc says `@throws pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.ExplorationException if corpus access fails`
Same pattern repeated for exploreMultiSeed (line 47), compareCollocateProfiles (line 58), fetchExamples (line 68) — all document throws in Javadoc but omit from signature
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByRelation (line 112): `throws ExplorationException` declared in signature

ExplorationHandlers catches ExplorationException via `catch (ExplorationException e)` on a reference of type ExplorationService — works at runtime since ExplorationException is unchecked, but IDE shows no throws declaration on the interface method, defeating the documented contract
findings.suggestion: Add `throws ExplorationException` to all four ExplorationService interface method signatures. Since ExplorationException extends RuntimeException it is syntactically permitted; adding it to the interface signature makes the documented exception contract visible to callers who hold an ExplorationService reference, consistent with what the implementation already declares.

findings.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeCqlQuery explicitly catches NullPointerException to re-throw it unchanged — catch-rethrow adds noise when the goal is only to prevent the generic RuntimeException handler below from wrapping it
evidence: Lines 152-154: `catch (NullPointerException e) { // Re-throw NPEs immediately — they are programming bugs, not expected runtime errors. throw e; }`
This is immediately followed by `catch (RuntimeException e) { throw new IOException("Unexpected error...", e); }` — the NPE handler exists purely to skip the wrapping in that handler
The pattern is unusual: a reader must understand the multi-catch ordering to see why NPE is singled out; the comment helps but the pattern itself is non-idiomatic
findings.suggestion: Replace the two-catch pattern with a single RuntimeException handler that checks for NullPointerException before wrapping: `catch (RuntimeException e) { if (e instanceof NullPointerException npe) throw npe; throw new IOException("Unexpected error executing CQL query: " + e.getMessage(), e); }`. Alternatively, move the NPE rethrow into an `instanceof`-guarded branch inside the single catch. Both alternatives read as intentional without relying on catch-clause ordering.

findings.summary: DiscoveredNoun and CoreCollocate record components carry no @NonNull/@Nullable annotations, inconsistent with WordSketchResult which annotates both key fields — nullability is undocumented for core domain model types
evidence: WordSketchResult (lines 7-8): `@NonNull String lemma`, `@NonNull String pos` — nullability explicitly documented
DiscoveredNoun: `String noun`, `Map<String, Double> sharedCollocates` — no annotations; a null noun would silently propagate into API responses (DiscoveredNounEntry.noun)
CoreCollocate: `String collocate` — no annotation; a null collocate flows into CoreCollocateEntry and then into JSON output

CollocateProfileComparator constructor `(QueryExecutor executor, GrammarConfig grammarConfig)` has no @NonNull/@Nullable on either parameter, unlike SemanticFieldExplorer's production constructors which annotate all parameters
findings.suggestion: Add `@NonNull` to the primary string identifier components of DiscoveredNoun (`noun`), CoreCollocate (`collocate`), and the Map components where null is not a valid value. Apply the same treatment to the `executor` parameter of CollocateProfileComparator's constructor. This aligns with the project's established jspecify annotation convention visible in WordSketchResult, QueryExecutor, and ExplorationService.

findings.summary: CollocateProfile-to-entry conversion loop repeated verbatim across assembler classes after split
evidence: collocateProfileToEntry loop pattern in ComparisonResponseAssembler identical structure to earlier ExploreResponseAssembler; the split preserved duplication without extracting a shared helper
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared CollocateEntryMapper utility or static method callable from both assemblers to eliminate the repeated Map<String,Double> nounScores building loop
